synthetic_oxydex_golden.node-export.json.note: ADDED 2026-07-12 (CPAP-REAL-CORPUS-2026-07-11-BRIEF §P9, resolving §M5) — a COMMITTED, content-addressed equivalence input for OxyDex. Before P9, every node's equivalence input was a REAL recording, hence gitignored, hence absent on a fresh clone: ALL EIGHT compute()-equivalence legs SKIPPED in CI. The suite's strongest correctness gate — the GATE-C surface that catches a code change which moved a fixture's OUTPUT — therefore ran only on the maintainer's machine, and a regression that moved an export would have gone green through CI. This input is SYNTHETIC (tools/make-synthetic-inputs.mjs): the vendor FORMAT is reproduced exactly, the DATA never is — closed-form, physiologically plausible waveforms containing no recording of any person and no device identifier. Deterministic (no RNG, no Date.now()), so re-running the generator reproduces it byte-for-byte and inputHashes stay stable. It ADDS to the real-recording leg (which still runs locally and exercises genuine vendor quirks); it does not replace it. REGENERATE the input and this golden together whenever the export shape changes.
